title: Installation
description: Set up a new CReact project from scratch. CReact runs on Node.js and uses JSX with TypeScript.
create_project_intro: Create a directory and initialize it:
code_init_project:
mkdir my-app && cd my-app
npm init -y
install_typescript_intro: Install TypeScript as a dev dependency:
configure_typescript_intro: Create a tsconfig.json with JSX configured for CReact:
tip_jsx_import_source: jsxImportSource tells TypeScript to use CReact's JSX runtime instead of React's, enabling <Component /> syntax.
add_scripts_intro: Update your package.json with a module type and run scripts:
create_entry_intro: Create index.tsx. CReact apps export a default async function that calls render() with a component, a Memory implementation, and a stack name:
run_outro: Your CReact app is now running. The --watch flag restarts on file changes.
code_project_structure:
my-app/
├── index.tsx           # Entry point, exports default async function
├── src/
│   ├── app.tsx         # Root component
│   ├── memory.ts       # Memory implementation (state persistence)
│   └── components/     # Your components
├── .state/             # Persisted state (auto-generated)
├── package.json
└── tsconfig.json
project_files_outro: The entry point (index.tsx) exports a default async function that the creact CLI calls. This function calls render() with your root component, a Memory implementation, and a stack name.
